# goal-cli

`goal-cli` keeps the user's thing in the center.

The user does not mainly care that an agent changed code. The user cares that
the thing they asked for is actually better: a PDF, website, slide deck, report,
chart pack, workbook, benchmark, app demo, or another result they can inspect.

Set up this project for goal-cli.

THE THING is the finished result the user will inspect.

If the user already named it, use that.

If not, infer it from the project.

Ask one question only if you cannot infer it safely.

Make future coding work prove THE THING is getting better.

If `goal-cli` is missing, install it from
https://github.com/SiyaoZheng/goal-cli with Python 3.11+.

Use `skills/goal-cli-project-setup/SKILL.md` if it is available.

Find the output file or runnable demo I can inspect. Create or update goal.toml
and any small rebuild script needed. Do not edit raw data, generated files,
.git/, or .goal/. Keep future write access narrow.

Run:

goal-cli validate
goal-cli doctor
goal-cli run --dry-run

After checks pass, recommend one timed heartbeat every 30 minutes:

goal-cli heartbeat install --every-minutes 30 --max-minutes 30

Report the output path, rebuild command, editable folders, protected folders,
whether to schedule the heartbeat, and the next command.

## Agent Entry Points

- [Project setup skill](skills/goal-cli-project-setup/SKILL.md): Use this to
  connect an existing project to `goal-cli`.
- [Template author skill](skills/goal-cli-template-author/SKILL.md): Use this
  only when improving reusable examples, checks, or docs in this repository.

## Core Documentation

- [README](README.md): Product overview and one-prompt quick start.
- [Installation](docs/installation.md): Install details and local setup checks.
- [goal.toml schema](docs/config-schema.md): Full configuration reference.
- [CLI reference](docs/cli-reference.md): Current command help.
- [Skills guide](docs/skills.md): Agent-facing setup instructions.

## Examples

- [PDF-first example](examples/scientificity/goal.toml): Research-paper setup
  where the thing is the rebuilt PDF.

## Optional

- [Thing-first design notes](docs/artifact-goal-notes.md): Why the final
  output is the control point.
- [Codex goal implementation report](docs/codex-goal-openai-implementation-report.md):
  Codex `/goal` integration details.
